lede: A look at October 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Giuseppe Verdi and Ivory Joe Hunter, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Armenia
events.desc: Armenia and Turkey sign the Zurich Protocols, intended to normalize relations. However, they are never ratified by either side.

events.title: Papua New Guinea
events.desc: Papua New Guinea joins the United Nations.

events.title: Spiro Agnew
events.desc: U.S. Vice President Spiro Agnew resigns after being charged with evasion of federal income tax.

events.title: 1964 Summer Olympics
events.desc: The Tokyo Summer Olympics opening ceremony is the first to be relayed live by satellites.

events.title: Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty
events.desc: The Partial Nuclear Test Ban Treaty comes into effect.

events.title: Triton (moon)
events.desc: Triton, the largest moon of the planet Neptune, is discovered by English astronomer William Lassell.

events.title: Ndyuka people
events.desc: In a treaty with the Dutch colonial authorities, the Ndyuka people of Suriname – descended from escaped slaves – gain territorial autonomy.

events.title: Christopher Columbus
events.desc: The crew of Christopher Columbus's ship, the Santa Maria, attempt a mutiny.

events.title: Charles Martel
events.desc: Charles Martel's forces defeat an Umayyad army near Tours, France.

births.name: AD 19
births.line: Roman son of Drusus Julius Caesar and Livilla; adoptive son of the Emperor Caligula

births.name: Harold Pinter
births.line: English playwright, screenwriter, director Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Naoto Kan
births.line: Japanese lawyer and politician, 61st Prime Minister of Japan

deaths.text: Orson Welles, American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter
Husayn ibn Ali, third Shia Imam and grandson of Muhammad
